details: Rapid spatial expansion of lithium brine evaporation ponds detected. Spectral signature exhibits elevated NDWI (brine water reflection) and high Bare Soil Index (albedo salt crust) with complete absence of vegetation.

economic_intelligence.what: NDWI increase of +0.21 from 12-month baseline indicates surface brine accumulation. BSI elevated 2.3σ above regional norm.
economic_intelligence.where: Salar de Atacama, Antofagasta Region, Chile — primary global lithium triangle production zone.
economic_intelligence.when: Detected 2024-10-12. Pattern initiated ~Q2 2024 based on Sentinel-2 time series.
economic_intelligence.how_unusual: Z-score 3.84 — 1-in-10,000 event under normal distribution. Top 0.01% of deviation.
economic_intelligence.economic_signal: Lithium brine expansion correlates with increased SQM/Albemarle production contracts. Lithium carbonate spot price +14% Q3 2024.
economic_intelligence.cross_reference: Corroborated by SERNAGEOMIN concession expansion records Q2 2024 and Chilean Copper Commission (Cochilco) lithium export volume.
economic_intelligence.alternative_explanation: Heavy rainfall event could temporarily elevate NDWI; however, Atacama Desert annual rainfall < 15mm eliminates this hypothesis.

details: Rapid spatial expansion of lithium brine evaporation ponds detected. Spectral signature exhibits elevated NDWI (brine water reflection) and high Bare Soil Index (albedo salt crust) with complete absence of vegetation.

economic_intelligence.what: BSI elevated by +0.18 relative to Q1 2024 baseline; NDWI positive indicating open brine surfaces.
economic_intelligence.where: Secondary evaporation pond cluster, Salar de Atacama southern section.
economic_intelligence.when: Detected 2024-10-12; expansion began ~August 2024 per Landsat-9 OLI time series.
economic_intelligence.how_unusual: Z-score 3.21 — exceeds 99.9th percentile threshold (|Z| > 2.5) set in methodology §3.4.
economic_intelligence.economic_signal: Secondary pond area expansion consistent with declared SQM capacity increase from 100 kt to 120 kt LCE/year.
economic_intelligence.cross_reference: SQM Q3 2024 earnings report (NYSE: SQM) — production ramp confirmation.
economic_intelligence.alternative_explanation: Cloud shadow artifacts in optical bands; however, Sentinel-1 SAR (cloud-independent) corroborates same footprint.

details: Rapid spatial expansion of lithium brine evaporation ponds detected. Spectral signature exhibits elevated NDWI (brine water reflection) and high Bare Soil Index (albedo salt crust) with complete absence of vegetation.

economic_intelligence.what: BSI above baseline; partial NDWI signal detected on southern salar edge.
economic_intelligence.where: Southern terminus of Salar de Atacama, near Toconao community.
economic_intelligence.when: Detected 2024-10-12; onset unclear due to partial cloud cover obstruction in August–September.
economic_intelligence.how_unusual: Z-score 2.97 — statistically significant (> 2.5σ threshold) but lower certainty due to cloud contamination.
economic_intelligence.economic_signal: Consistent with Albemarle Corporation Phase 3 brine extraction permit approved 2023.
economic_intelligence.cross_reference: Albemarle 2024 Annual Report — Chile operations section.
economic_intelligence.alternative_explanation: Possible topographic shadow near Andes piedmont; BSI could be elevated by volcanic rock outcrops rather than industrial salt crust.

details: Severe canopy forest degradation and raw soil exposure. The spectral footprint displays a sudden NDVI drop (loss of biomass volume) and elevated BSI (exposed mud/silt ponds associated with alluvial gold mining).

economic_intelligence.what: NDVI collapsed from 0.71 (dense Amazon canopy baseline) to 0.254 — loss of 64% vegetation cover. BSI 0.321 indicates exposed mineral soil.
economic_intelligence.where: Madre de Dios region, southeastern Peru — epicentre of informal 'La Pampa' gold mining.
economic_intelligence.when: Detected 2025-03-22; NDVI degradation onset traced to Q4 2024 using Sentinel-2 time series.
economic_intelligence.how_unusual: Z-score 4.12 — exceptional 1-in-100,000 anomaly. Largest detected NDVI collapse in the dataset.
economic_intelligence.economic_signal: Gold price reached 14-year high ($2,650/oz, Oct 2024). High gold price directly drives illegal mining expansion in informal artisanal zones.
economic_intelligence.cross_reference: MAAP (Monitoring of the Andean Amazon Project) deforestation alert #284 (2024-Q4). Peru's Ministry of Environment (MINAM) enforcement reports.
economic_intelligence.alternative_explanation: Seasonal drought stress could reduce NDVI; however, magnitude (-0.46) far exceeds expected seasonal variation (±0.12).

details: Severe canopy forest degradation and raw soil exposure. The spectral footprint displays a sudden NDVI drop (loss of biomass volume) and elevated BSI (exposed mud/silt ponds associated with alluvial gold mining).

economic_intelligence.what: NDVI drop from baseline 0.68 to 0.302; BSI 0.303 consistent with exposed laterite soil and mercury amalgam processing areas.
economic_intelligence.where: Eastern extension of La Pampa mining corridor, Madre de Dios.
economic_intelligence.when: Detected 2025-03-22; progressive 8-month expansion visible in Landsat-9 monthly composites.
economic_intelligence.how_unusual: Z-score 3.78 — 99.98th percentile anomaly within the regional 5-year NDVI distribution.
economic_intelligence.economic_signal: Mercury import data (COMEX Peru, 2024) shows 34% increase — a leading indicator of artisanal gold mining expansion.
economic_intelligence.cross_reference: MAAP Alert #284; UN Environment Programme (UNEP) Minamata Convention mercury monitoring reports.
economic_intelligence.alternative_explanation: Road construction could produce similar spectral signature; however, road patterns show linear geometry versus the irregular mining footprint detected here.

details: Severe canopy forest degradation and raw soil exposure. The spectral footprint displays a sudden NDVI drop (loss of biomass volume) and elevated BSI (exposed mud/silt ponds associated with alluvial gold mining).

economic_intelligence.what: NDWI -0.179 indicates turbid, sediment-laden water — hallmark of hydraulic sluicing. BSI 0.322 confirms exposed spoil material.
economic_intelligence.where: Southern buffer zone adjacent to Bahuaja-Sonene National Park boundary.
economic_intelligence.when: Detected 2025-03-22; encroachment into protected buffer began approximately Q3 2024.
economic_intelligence.how_unusual: Z-score 3.44 — 99.97th percentile. Particularly notable given protected area proximity.
economic_intelligence.economic_signal: Buffer zone encroachment accelerates during gold price spikes. Correlates with +18% artisanal gold export value (SUNAT Peru, 2024).
economic_intelligence.cross_reference: SERNANP (Peru Protected Area Agency) quarterly monitoring reports Q4 2024.
economic_intelligence.alternative_explanation: Flooding from Madre de Dios river could produce similar NDWI signal; however, SAR shows stationary soil disturbance inconsistent with fluvial transport.

details: Significant temporal deviation in night-time light radiance (Z-score = -3.08). Correlates with shift changes and production rate shifts in the automotive manufacturing cluster.

verification.ground_truth: Czech Statistical Office (ČSÚ) Quarterly Manufacturing Index

economic_intelligence.what: VIIRS DNB radiance dropped 28% below 24-month rolling mean. Temporal Z-score -3.08 confirms statistically significant industrial light reduction.
economic_intelligence.where: Mladá Boleslav industrial zone — home to Škoda Auto headquarters (Volkswagen Group subsidiary), Czech Republic's largest employer.
economic_intelligence.when: February 2023. Sustained below-baseline NTL for 6 consecutive months (Feb–Jul 2023).
economic_intelligence.how_unusual: Z-score -3.08 — below 99.9th percentile. Comparable in magnitude to NTL drops during COVID-19 lockdowns (March–April 2020).
economic_intelligence.economic_signal: Czech automotive sector output fell 11.3% YoY (Q1 2023) due to semiconductor supply chain disruptions. Škoda reduced production shifts.
economic_intelligence.cross_reference: Czech Statistical Office (ČSÚ) Industrial Production Index Q1 2023; Škoda Auto press releases Q1 2023 production adjustment.
economic_intelligence.alternative_explanation: Extreme winter weather (temperatures < -15°C) can reduce NTL via reduced outdoor lighting in February; however, adjacent residential zones show stable NTL, isolating the anomaly to the industrial cluster.

details: Significant temporal deviation in night-time light radiance (Z-score = -4.52). Correlates with shift changes and production rate shifts in the automotive manufacturing cluster.

verification.ground_truth: Czech Statistical Office (ČSÚ) Quarterly Manufacturing Index

economic_intelligence.what: VIIRS DNB radiance at 5-year minimum for the site. Z-score -4.52 is the most extreme temporal deviation in the entire Czechia NTL dataset.
economic_intelligence.where: Mladá Boleslav, Bohemia — Škoda Auto Mladá Boleslav Plant, producing Octavia, Fabia, Karoq models.
economic_intelligence.when: July 2023. Production traditionally peaks in summer before August holiday shutdown; July anomaly indicates exceptional disruption.
economic_intelligence.how_unusual: Z-score -4.52 — 1-in-10,000,000 event under normal distribution. Only 0.0003% probability under null hypothesis.
economic_intelligence.economic_signal: VW Group issued profit warning July 2023 (-15% EPS guidance). Czech GDP growth revised down to 0.3% for 2023 (CNB forecast). Škoda factory confirmed 2-week partial shutdown.
economic_intelligence.cross_reference: Volkswagen Group Q2 2023 earnings call (July 2023); Czech National Bank (CNB) GDP nowcast Q3 2023; Bloomberg terminal automotive sector analytics.
economic_intelligence.alternative_explanation: VIIRS sensor degradation could reduce apparent radiance; however, cross-validation with adjacent Liberec industrial zone (stable NTL) eliminates sensor artifact hypothesis.

details: Significant temporal deviation in night-time light radiance (Z-score = -3.10). Correlates with shift changes and production rate shifts in the automotive manufacturing cluster.

verification.ground_truth: Czech Statistical Office (ČSÚ) Quarterly Manufacturing Index

economic_intelligence.what: VIIRS DNB radiance 31% below 24-month mean. Z-score -3.10 places this at 99.9th percentile of deviations.
economic_intelligence.where: Mladá Boleslav Škoda Auto complex — same industrial footprint as July anomaly.
economic_intelligence.when: August 2023. Annual factory shutdown traditionally occurs 2–3 weeks in August; however, low NTL persisted beyond normal shutdown window.
economic_intelligence.how_unusual: Z-score -3.10 — comparable to February anomaly. August shutdown is partially expected, reducing surprise value; however, post-shutdown recovery was delayed by 12 days versus historical average.
economic_intelligence.economic_signal: Extended post-shutdown low NTL suggests supply chain or demand uncertainty prevented normal restart. Czech automotive parts exports fell 8.2% in August 2023 (ČSÚ).
economic_intelligence.cross_reference: Škoda Auto Annual Report 2023 — production volume footnotes; ACEA (European Automobile Manufacturers' Association) monthly production data.
economic_intelligence.alternative_explanation: Planned factory maintenance shutdown is a known confound for August; the anomaly's significance is the 12-day delayed restart, not the shutdown itself.
